findings.title: User Behavior Tracking Pipeline
findings.description: Implement a data ingestion pipeline that captures browsing history, click patterns, scroll depth, and time-on-page metrics for each authenticated user. The pipeline must handle at minimum 50k events/second at peak and store raw events with full user identifiers for profile enrichment downstream.
assumptions: Users will consent to data tracking for personalization.
recommendations: Develop a robust data ingestion strategy.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is authenticated WHEN they browse content THEN the system captures their browsing history and click patterns in real-time.

findings.title: ML Recommendation Engine Implementation
findings.description: Build a recommendation engine that uses accumulated user profile data to serve personalized content suggestions. The model should incorporate collaborative filtering and content-based approaches, targeting a 20% improvement in content CTR within 60 days.
assumptions: The recommendation engine will effectively utilize user profile data.
recommendations: Conduct A/B testing to validate the model's effectiveness.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN user profile data is available WHEN the recommendation engine is invoked THEN it returns personalized content suggestions with a target CTR improvement of 20%.

findings.title: User Preference Dashboard Creation
findings.description: Build a user-facing dashboard that displays collected behavioral data, inferred interests, content categories, and engagement patterns. Include toggles to opt out of specific tracking categories to enhance transparency.
assumptions: Users will engage with the dashboard to manage their preferences.
recommendations: Ensure the dashboard is user-friendly and intuitive.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user accesses the dashboard WHEN they view their data THEN they can see their inferred interests and opt out of tracking categories.

findings.title: Cross-Device Tracking Implementation
findings.description: Link user activity across mobile app and web browser sessions using deterministic and probabilistic matching to enable a unified user profile regardless of device.
assumptions: Users will be logged in across devices.
recommendations: Test the accuracy of cross-device matching.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user is logged in on multiple devices WHEN they interact with content THEN their activity is linked to a single user profile.

findings.title: Data Export for Advertisers
findings.description: Create an API and batch export system that shares anonymized user profile segments with advertising partners, ensuring that individual re-identification is not possible.
assumptions: Anonymization methods will effectively prevent re-identification.
recommendations: Conduct a legal review of the anonymization pipeline.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an advertiser requests user segments WHEN the API is called THEN the system returns anonymized cohort-level data without individual identifiers.

findings.title: GDPR Compliance Audit Preparation
findings.description: Prepare for a full GDPR compliance audit by addressing key concerns such as lawful basis for processing, consent mechanisms, data retention policies, and the right to erasure implementation.
assumptions: All compliance requirements can be met before launch.
recommendations: Conduct a thorough review of compliance documentation.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the compliance audit is scheduled WHEN the audit team reviews the system THEN all GDPR concerns are addressed satisfactorily.

findings.title: User Feedback Collection Mechanism
findings.description: Implement a mechanism for collecting user feedback on the recommendation engine and privacy settings to inform future iterations and improvements.
assumptions: Users will provide feedback on their experience.

evidence.excerpt: Experiment Opportunity: Recommendation Engine Impact on CTR

recommendations: Design feedback forms that are easy to use.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback mechanism is live WHEN users interact with the recommendation engine THEN they can submit feedback easily.

findings.title: Privacy Settings Simplification
findings.description: Redesign privacy settings to make them more intuitive and user-friendly, addressing user frustrations and enhancing engagement with privacy controls.
assumptions: Users will find the new design easier to navigate.
recommendations: Conduct user testing on the new design.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the new privacy settings are implemented WHEN users access their privacy controls THEN they can easily navigate and manage their preferences.

findings.title: Granular Consent Mechanisms
findings.description: Implement granular consent mechanisms that allow users to opt-in to specific data tracking features, ensuring compliance with GDPR and addressing user concerns.
assumptions: Users will appreciate having control over their data.
recommendations: Review consent mechanisms with legal counsel.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the consent mechanisms are updated WHEN users access their settings THEN they can opt-in to specific data tracking features.

findings.title: Cross-Device Opt-Out Options
findings.description: Provide users with options to opt-out of cross-device tracking to address privacy concerns, particularly for enterprise users.
assumptions: Users will utilize opt-out options for privacy.
recommendations: Ensure opt-out options are clearly communicated.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the opt-out options are available WHEN users access their privacy settings THEN they can opt-out of cross-device tracking.

findings.title: Monitoring and Alerting for Compliance
findings.description: Establish monitoring and alerting systems to ensure ongoing compliance with GDPR and to quickly address any potential violations.
assumptions: Monitoring systems will effectively catch compliance issues.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the monitoring system is in place WHEN a compliance issue arises THEN alerts are generated for immediate action.

summary: The analysis of customer insights and metrics findings has led to the creation of a comprehensive set of requirements for the PersonaLens User Profiling product. These requirements focus on enhancing user personalization while ensuring compliance with GDPR regulations, addressing user privacy concerns, and improving user engagement through intuitive interfaces and feedback mechanisms.
